steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the excavation process. The passage tells us why: it 'can take weeks or even months of hard, physically tiring work.' The word must capture that this process demands significant effort and time.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cheap: Cost is never mentioned in the passage. Nothing about weeks of labor implies anything about expense. ✗
  • Choice B — common: Frequency is not addressed. The passage describes the nature of the work, not how often it occurs. ✗
  • Choice C — rapid: Directly contradicted by the passage — taking weeks or months is the opposite of rapid. ✗
  • Choice D — difficult: Weeks or months of hard, physically tiring work is precisely what 'difficult' means in context. The duration and physical demand both point here. ✓
That matches Choice D — difficult.
